use tonic::transport::Channel;
use tonic::Request;

struct ServiceClient {
    client: my_service::MyServiceClient<Channel>,
}

impl ServiceClient {
    async fn connect(addr: &str) -> Result<Self, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        let channel = Channel::from_shared(addr.to_string())?
            .connect()
            .await?;
        let client = my_service::MyServiceClient::new(channel);
        Ok(Self { client })
    }

    async fn say_hello(&mut self, name: &str) -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
        let request = Request::new(my_service::HelloRequest { name: name.to_string() });
        let response = self.client.say_hello(request).await?;
        Ok(response.into_inner().message)
    }
}

async fn batch_call(client: &mut ServiceClient, names: &[&str]) -> Vec<String> {
    let mut results = Vec::new();
    for name in names {
        let result = client.say_hello(name).await.unwrap();
        results.push(result);
    }
    results
}

mod my_service {
    tonic::include_proto!("my_service");
    pub struct HelloRequest { pub name: String }
    pub struct HelloResponse { pub message: String }
    pub struct MyServiceClient<T>(T);
    impl<T> MyServiceClient<T> {
        pub fn new(_t: T) -> Self { Self(_t) }
        pub async fn say_hello(&mut self, _req: tonic::Request<HelloRequest>) -> Result<tonic::Response<HelloResponse>, tonic::Status> { todo!() }
    }
}
